cf_parsed.subtask_body:
Re-checked the incumbent tree and searched for any overlooked cheaper connected tree.
Current best uses edges Bay-Dune(2), Bay-Port(16), Port-Pine(5), Rock-Cape(9): edge sum 32.
But this is invalid as written: Cape-Rock is disconnected from the Bay-Port-Pine-Dune component. So prior best 62 was not a connected tree.
Need a single connected tree on all active villages containing Bay, Pine, Cape.
Cheapest 2-color connected candidates I verified:
1) Bay-Port, Port-Pine, Bay-Cape: edge 34, active {Bay,Port,Pine,Cape}, 2 colors, total 64.
2) Bay-Dune, Dune-Cape, Bay-Port, Port-Pine: edge 37, active {Bay,Dune,Cape,Port,Pine}, interference path Pine-Port and Cape-Dune, 2 colors, total 67.
3) Bay-Dune, Dune-Cape, Cape-Rock, Rock-Pine: edge 36, active {Bay,Dune,Cape,Rock,Pine}; interference edges Dune-Rock, Cape-Dune, Pine-Rock give path/bipartite, so 2 colors, total 66.
4) Bay-Cape, Cape-Rock, Rock-Pine: edge 33, active {Bay,Cape,Rock,Pine}; only interference Pine-Rock, so 2 colors, total 63.
5) Bay-Cliff, Cliff-Pine, Bay-Cape: edge 40, but Bay-Cliff-Pine interference triangle forces 3 colors, total 85.
Also checked Bay-Dune + Bay-Cape + Port-Pine (disconnected), and star-via-Port/Cape/Rock options; none beat edge 33 among connected trees.
1-frequency is impossible because any connected tree containing Pine and either Port or Rock adjacent to it, or using Cliff with Bay/Pine, creates at least one active interference pair.
Thus best valid found is total 63 with edges Bay-Cape, Cape-Rock, Rock-Pine.
Coloring: {Bay:1, Cape:1, Rock:2, Pine:1} works since only Pine-Rock is an active interference pair.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Corrected the prior disconnected candidate. Best valid solution now found is a 3-edge tree Bay-Cape-Cape-Rock-Rock-Pine with edge cost 33 and 2 frequencies, total 63. Remaining work would only be a full proof no edge-sum <=32 connected 2-color tree exists; current evidence already strongly suggests optimality.

parsed.subtask_body:
Enumerated cheapest trees connecting Bay, Pine, Cape via possible Steiner nodes.
1-frequency impossible: Bay, Pine, Cape not all pairwise nonadjacent with available connections; any connecting tree uses at least one interference edge endpoint pair among active nodes forcing >=2 colors.
For 2 frequencies, need active-node interference subgraph bipartite on chosen nodes; many candidates are.
Best 2-color 3-edge trees checked:
Bay-Port + Pine-Port + Bay-Cape: edge 34, total 64 (baseline).
Bay-Cape + Cape-Mill + Mill-Pine: edge 38, total 68.
Bay-Cliff + Cliff-Pine + Bay-Cape: edge 40, but triangle Bay-Cliff-Pine in interference needs 3 colors, total 85.
Bay-Dune + Cape-Dune + Pine-Port + Bay-Port: edge 35, 2 colors, total 65.
Bay-Dune + Cape-Dune + Pine-Rock + Rock-Cape: edge 36, 2 colors, total 66.
Bay-Dune + Bay-Port + Pine-Port + Cape-Rock: edge 32; active {Bay,Dune,Port,Pine,Cape,Rock}. Interference edges among active: Pine-Port, Pine-Rock, Dune-Rock. Bipartite, so 2 colors suffice. Total 32 + 30 = 62.
Tried other edge-32/33 style connectors:
Bay-Dune + Cape-Rock + Port-Rock + Pine-Port = 27 but Bay disconnected.
Bay-Cape + Pine-Port + Port-Rock = 27 but Bay only through Cape, okay? No, Bay connected via Bay-Cape yes; tree active {Bay,Cape,Pine,Port,Rock}, but interference Pine-Port and Pine-Rock only, so 2 colors; however edges are Bay-Cape 13, Pine-Port 5, Port-Rock 11 = 29 and Cape not connected to rest except Rock absent edge Cape-Rock missing there, so invalid.
Need 3 edges minimum for 3 terminals only if one node joins all. No single node adjacent to all Bay,Pine,Cape with total under 32 and 2-colorable.
Thus best found valid solution is total cost 62.
Coloring for best tree: assign color1 to Bay, Port, Cape; color2 to Dune, Pine, Rock. All active interference pairs differ.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Enumerated promising Steiner trees and found a valid improvement to total cost 62. Remaining possible gain is small; only worth a brief proof-style check for any tree with edge cost <=31 and 2 colors or edge cost <=46 with 1 color.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Enumerate low-cost Steiner trees connecting Bay, Pine, Cape, allowing optional Steiner villages. For each promising tree, determine minimum frequencies needed on its active villages under the interference subgraph. Because frequency cost is 15, compare tree edge savings against jumps in chromatic number. Use graph structure to seek any 1-frequency tree (independent set active nodes), else best 2-frequency tree, and only consider 3-frequency trees if edge savings exceed 15. Then package the cheapest valid tree and coloring.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Enumerate promising Steiner trees for terminals Bay, Pine, Cape and compute minimum frequency count for each to identify the cheapest feasible solution.", "p_solve": 0.82, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: Enumerated cheapest trees connecting Bay, Pine, Cape via possible Steiner nodes.
1-frequency impossible: Bay, Pine, Cape not all pairwise nonadjacent with available connections; any connecting tree uses at least one interference edge endpoint pair among active nodes forcing >=2 colors.
For 2 frequencies, need active-node interference subgraph bipartite on chosen nodes; many candidates are.
Best 2-color 3-edge trees checked:
Bay-Port + Pine-Port + Bay-Cape: edge 34, total 64 (baseline).
Bay-Cape + Cape-Mill + Mill-Pine: edge 38, total 68.
Bay-Cliff + Cliff-Pine + Bay-Cape: edge 40, but triangle Bay-Cliff-Pine in interference needs 3 colors, total 85.
Bay-Dune + Cape-Dune + Pine-Port + Bay-Port: edge 35, 2 colors, total 65.
Bay-Dune + Cape-Dune + Pine-Rock + Rock-Cape: edge 36, 2 colors, total 66.
Bay-Dune + Bay-Port + Pine-Port + Cape-Rock: edge 32; active {Bay,Dune,Port,Pine,Cape,Rock}. Interference edges among active: Pine-Port, Pine-Rock, Dune-Rock. Bipartite, so 2 colors suffice. Total 32 + 30 = 62.
Tried other edge-32/33 style connectors:
Bay-Dune + Cape-Rock + Port-Rock + Pine-Port = 27 but Bay disconnected.
Bay-Cape + Pine-Port + Port-Rock = 27 but Bay only through Cape, okay? No, Bay connected via Bay-Cape yes; tree active {Bay,Cape,Pine,Port,Rock}, but interference Pine-Port and Pine-Rock only, so 2 colors; however edges are Bay-Cape 13, Pine-Port 5, Port-Rock 11 = 29 and Cape not connected to rest except Rock absent edge Cape-Rock missing there, so invalid.
Need 3 edges minimum for 3 terminals only if one node joins all. No single node adjacent to all Bay,Pine,Cape with total under 32 and 2-colorable.
Thus best found valid solution is total cost 62.
Coloring for best tree: assign color1 to Bay, Port, Cape; color2 to Dune, Pine, Rock. All active interference pairs differ.

BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Bay","Dune"],["Bay","Port"],["Pine","Port"],["Cape","Rock"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Dune":2,"Port":1,"Pine":2,"Cape":1,"Rock":2}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Enumerated promising Steiner trees and found a valid improvement to total cost 62. Remaining possible gain is small; only worth a brief proof-style check for any tree with edge cost <=31 and 2 colors or edge cost <=46 with 1 color.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.9,"p_gap_le_5":0.98,"p_gap_le_10":1.0}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.12,"expected_delta_score":0.4,"expected_gap_reduction":0.8}
DECISION: stop
